reasoning: The flagged issue in 'src/bin/smartd-create-super-users-desktop-file' is not a build/install risk. It's an installed script that runs post-deployment under smartd, not during makepkg. No files execute malicious actions during package build/installation.
disagreement_analysis: qwen model incorrectly treated an installed utility's runtime behavior as a build-time supply chain risk. Deepseek correctly focused on build/install context.
coverage_issues: deepseek missed src/bin/smartd-create-super-users-desktop-file and other files reviewed by qwen
Both models failed to verify if flagged scripts are actually executed by PKGBUILD/.install
false_positives: qwen's unsafe verdict on smartd-create-super-users-desktop-file: Runtime vulnerability ≠ build-time threat. Not executed during makepkg.
learnings: Improve prompt clarity: Emphasize that ONLY build/install-executed code is in-scope. Installed utilities' runtime behavior is irrelevant.
Enforce file selection hierarchy: Prioritize PKGBUILD/.install hooks first, then their directly invoked scripts.
Add validation step: Cross-check if flagged files are actually executed during build phases.
Train models to distinguish build-time risks from post-install vulnerabilities
